document_id: 4.REGULATION (EU) 20241689 OF THE EUROPEAN PARLIAMENT AND OF THE COUNCIL.html
category:
Category: Privacy

Explanation: This appears to be the EU AI Act, which primarily focuses on regulating artificial intelligence systems to protect fundamental rights including privacy and personal data protection. The reference to Article 16 of the TFEU (which deals with data protection) in the legal basis and emphasis on protecting individual rights indicates this is primarily a privacy-focused regulation.

extracted_info.title: Regulation (EU) 2024/1689 - Artificial Intelligence Act
extracted_info.regulatory_body: European Commission and National Competent Authorities
extracted_info.scope: AI systems placed on the market, put into service, or used in the European Union, including general-purpose AI models

key_requirement_details.requirement: Registration of high-risk AI systems in EU database
key_requirement_details.deadline: Before placing on market or putting into service
key_requirement_details.compliance_action: Register system details in EU database

reporting_requirements.frequency: Immediately and no later than 15 days after awareness

monetary_thresholds.description: Maximum administrative fine for prohibited AI practices
Maximum administrative fine for non-compliance with other obligations
Maximum administrative fine for incorrect information to authorities

phased_milestones: 2025-08-02: Chapter III Section 4, Chapter V, Chapter VII and Chapter XII apply

notes: The regulation creates a comprehensive framework for AI governance in the EU
Specific focus on high-risk AI systems and general-purpose AI models
Creates new institutional structures including the AI Office and European AI Board
Introduces mandatory requirements for high-risk AI systems
